title: VIDEO Are there any plans to re release End of Eva
display_title: VIDEO Are there any plans to re release End of Eva
wikitext:
[]: [VIDEO] Are there any plans to re-release End of Eva?             Quote Post by HENRCAST » Mon Jan 12, 2009 10:16 pm After buying the thinpak, I wanted End of Evangelion, and found out that the only american version ever released wasn't remastered and already out-of-print. So being a non-remastered and expensive DVD, and decided not to have all the trouble of importing it (I live in Brazil). I mean, if you can download the remastered version what is the point of wasting money on the DVD?. I'm aware that EoE wasn't licensed by ADV, and I can't help but to think that the company that licensed it sucks.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jan 12, 2009 11:00 pm In the summer of 2006, there was word that Manga Entertainment had licensed the Renewal versions of the "Evangelion movies" and would be putting them out on DVD. You can see how far that's gone.

[]: Re: Are there any plans to re-release End of Eva?             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Jan 13, 2009 7:15 pm HENRCAST wrote: I'm aware that EoE wasn't licensed by ADV, and I can't help but to think that the company that licensed it sucks.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Jan 17, 2009 8:28 am I'd wait until Japan gets it out on Blu-ray.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Sat Jan 17, 2009 12:44 pm It unlikey now, the best choice is to get the game used on Amazon for $20.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jan 17, 2009 1:35 pm Don't you mean DVD? Anyway, you could always just get the Japanese Release of Renewal Feature Films.... http://www.cdjapan.co.jp/detailview.html?KEY=KIBA-1009

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Sat Jan 17, 2009 10:55 pm I think Mangle has been holding onto them so that they can release them around the same time as Rebuild of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun Jan 18, 2009 12:07 pm ^good theory there

[]:             Quote Post by HENRCAST » Mon Jan 19, 2009 3:49 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Don't you mean DVD? Anyway, you could always just get the Japanese Release of Renewal Feature Films.... http://www.cdjapan.co.jp/detailview.html?KEY=KIBA-1009

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Jan 19, 2009 8:13 pm HENRCAST wrote: I'd buy it, but as it seems, it doesn't have english subtitles.... At least, the cover looks awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 19, 2009 11:54 pm HENRCAST wrote: At least, the cover looks awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Tue Jan 20, 2009 10:00 am Since ADV films transferred the licenses to over 30 different shows into the hands of Funimation which means Funimation might get the rights to the Renewal versions of the Evangelion movies since Funimation now owns the rights to the series (I might be wrong though). If End of Evangelion comes to Blu Ray then I will be one happy person since the movies will look amazing on a HD TV!

[]:             Quote Post by HENRCAST » Tue Jan 20, 2009 10:47 am Guyver Spawn wrote: If End of Evangelion comes to Blu Ray then I will be one happy person since the movies will look amazing on a HD TV!

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Tue Jan 20, 2009 12:42 pm ^I don't know but the series coming to Blu ray along with the movies is possible since most animes are coming out on Blu Ray. Since Funimation has most of ADV films licenses it possible that they will re-release the platinum collection on Blu Ray. Funimation already has plans to put Dragon Ball Z/GT, One Piece and a few other animes on Blu Ray.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Mon Feb 02, 2009 2:15 pm Guyver Spawn wrote: Since ADV films transferred the licenses to over 30 different shows into the hands of Funimation which means Funimation might get the rights to the Renewal versions of the Evangelion movies since Funimation now owns the rights to the series

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Feb 04, 2009 1:02 am It be nice to have a re-release. I'm really disappointed in the image quality on the Manga DVD. I had to torrent a copy offline that is actually a little bit clearer. ALAS whoever made the torrent cut off a very portion of the edge of the screen. Still, its sad when a torrent gives you better quality than a DVD. Especially now that said DVD sells for like $50 on Ebay. Glad I got it before it went missing.

[]:             Quote Post by Evayo » Mon Mar 16, 2009 12:10 am A Blu-REI edition would be great, no matter who has the rights to it. ~:Evayo:~
